comment: Sandbox interaction kit — the valve->spout->jug->pedestal->beacon chain plus grab/drop/throw + the box-stack reach, expressed entirely as DATA. This is the reference semantics consumed by InteractionInterpreter; it reproduces the hand-wired scripts/interaction/*.gd exactly (parity gate: tests/interaction_behavior_test.gd, 6/6). See docs/decisions/affordance-substrate.md. PROMPT GRAMMAR (pinned, closed): a prompt is EITHER a literal string with {field} / {pct field} interpolation over readable state, OR a list of guarded variants [{when?, text}] resolved first-match (absent when = default). NO inline ternary expressions. {field} reads a state field on the verb's prompt scope (self for command/grab; held for place verbs read held.<field>); {pct field} renders round(field*100). See InteractionInterpreter._render_prompt.

tick.comment: While the valve flows AND a jug overlaps the stream, fill it a deterministic fill_rate*dt. No bespoke _physics_process; a tick effect gated by a while guard.

prompt.text: [E] Pick up jug    ({pct fill}% — fill at spout)

reactions.comment: Arm on the pedestal's activation event (replaces signal activated/connect).

tick.comment: Convergence AND-gate as data: armed (interaction chain) AND the player body reached the beacon region (movement chain). Unarmed reach is inert because the guard is false. Idempotent trigger.

interactables.comment: SLICE 3 — a brand-new dense interactable authored PURELY AS DATA (no engine change), the interaction analogue of movement's bullet-jump payoff. A second convergence puzzle that mirrors the beacon's armed-AND-reached gate with a DIFFERENT pair of independently-composed chains: a LEVER (a stateful command toggle, like the valve) AND a PRESSURE PLATE held down by a placed/standing weight (a box overlapping the plate's Area3D region, read via in_region — the same physics-seam guard the spout uses). The GATE opens only on the AND of both, and (unlike the latching beacon) it is a LIVE gate: it closes again the moment either condition drops, proving the AND-gate composition generalizes in both directions. Every verb/guard/effect/region used here ALREADY existed before this slice: command verb, toggle_state, emit, in_region(tag), set_state(true/false), state_bool, all/not, ref:<name> cross-object reads, region decl. ZERO new primitives.

PRESSURE PLATE — pressed is DATA derived from a placed/standing weight: while a box (grabbable_box) overlaps the plate's Area3D region, set pressed=true; otherwise set it false. Two complementary tick entries (in_region / not in_region) make `pressed` a faithful, deterministic projection of the weight overlap. The overlap test itself is engine physics (the once-per-tick frame); the affordance — what 'a weight holds the plate down' MEANS — is data.

tick.comment: A weight on the pad presses it.
No weight -> the plate springs back up.

interactables.comment: GATE — the SECOND convergence AND-gate, the densest node of Slice 3. open iff (lever.thrown AND plate.pressed), read cross-object via two refs. Unlike the beacon's idempotent latch, this is a LIVE gate: a second tick closes it (open=false) the moment EITHER chain drops, so the converse-is-inert property is proven continuously, not just at first contact. lever.thrown comes from the lever's command chain; plate.pressed comes from the placed-weight chain — two independently-composed inputs converging in one all-guard, exactly as the beacon's armed AND reached, but with an entirely different upstream pair.

tick.comment: Either chain drops -> close (live gate; the converse of the AND is inert by construction).
